talking_points: U.S. stocks fell this week — S&P 500 -0.5% (1-month -1.6%); Nasdaq -1.9%, small caps +0.3%.
Sectors: Health Care led (+7.1%), Technology lagged (-3.5%).
Rates: 10y Treasury at 4.38%, 2s10s 0.28. Fed funds 3.5–3.75%; the 1y market implies ~31.0 bps over 12m.
Breadth: 65% of the S&P 500 is above its 50-day average (63% above the 200-day).
Options: SPY dealer gamma is positive (vol-dampening) — dealers cushion moves.

fed_funds.note: Implied path is a rough proxy (1Y UST minus effective fed funds). Full FedWatch probabilities require CME data (paid).
